selected_important_turn.text: Same here. I'll go ahead and list some of what I need and you let me know if you will need the same as well. I need some extra water for our camping trip as well as some additional food.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first explicit statement of YOUR needs (water and food) and opens the conversation to integrative tradeoffs. Sharing preferences at this early stage set the agenda for possible cooperative exchanges.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The message is useful but vague — it doesn't specify quantities, relative priority between water and food, or ask about firewood. Missing those specifics leaves room for misunderstandings and misses a chance to surface potential complementarities (e.g., if the opponent values firewood more).
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Be more specific and diagnostic: state how many units you ideally want, rank the items by priority (or indicate willingness to trade one for another), and ask an explicit question about the other person's firewood needs and total counts. That invites precise, integrative proposals rather than guessing.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — more precise info opens additional integrative trades and reduces wasted proposals.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — clearer priorities help reach allocations aligned with needs.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — reduces back-and-forth by clarifying what each party actually wants.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: slight increase — clarity signals cooperativeness and reduces friction.

value2reason.Low: The temperature might drop during the night and I want to keep my camp warm.
value2reason.Medium: I am allergic to some of the food that was provided so I need additional choices.
value2reason.High: One of my family members decided to join us last minute and now we no longer have enough water for everyone.

value2reason.Low: Food is more of a luxury instead of a necessity. You need water to survive.
value2reason.Medium: It might be hot outside which leads to thirst. Water is important for staying hydrated.
value2reason.High: Firewood is needed because campfires are pleasant and firewood only lasts a finite amount of time

dialogue.text: Hello there I was just thinking of the upcoming camping trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm sorry about that, accidentally hit that send button lol! I was thinking about it as well. I'm pretty excited. How about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I was just thinking about the logistics and how to prioritize the various items.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Same here. I'll go ahead and list some of what I need and you let me know if you will need the same as well. I need some extra water for our camping trip as well as some additional food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm more focused on firewood since campfires are really pleasant and we don't want to run out. How about you? What do you need?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 water packages and 1 food package for myself. Water is my top priority, followed by food. How many firewood packages do you need, and are you open to trading some firewood for extra water or food?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm looking for at least 2 firewood packages for myself. I'm open to trading some water or food if you need more firewood. What's your stance on this?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm aiming for 2 water and 1 food for myself, but I'm flexible. If you need more firewood, I'm open to trading 1 water or food for 1 firewood. What’s your ideal split?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m aiming for 2 firewood packages for myself. I’m open to trading 1 water or food for 1 firewood if you need more. What’s your ideal split?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m aiming for 2 water and 1 food for myself, but I’m flexible. If you need more firewood, I’m open to trading 1 water or food for 1 firewood. What’s your ideal split?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm aiming for 2 firewood packages for myself. I'm open to trading 1 water or food for 1 firewood if you need more. What’s your ideal split?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I value water the most, followed by food, then firewood. I’m aiming for 2 water and 1 food for myself, but I’m open to trading 1 water or food for 1 firewood if needed. What’s your ideal split?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m willing to settle for 2 firewood, 1 water, and 1 food for myself. That way, I get my priority items while still being fair. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think that split could work. Let me confirm: you take 2 firewood, 1 water, and 1 food, and I take the remaining 1 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood. That way, we both get our priorities met and the total is split fairly.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That split works for me. Final allocation: you get 2 firewood, 1 water, and 1 food; I get 1 firewood, 2 water, and 2 food. Let’s head out and enjoy the trip!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
